text:
Shihael (シハエル, Shihaeru) Hunter × Hunter Hunter Association Official Issue: Hunter's Guide; Character & World Official Databook (pg. 204) was a Greed Island player. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 15, Chapter 132 

Appearance

Shihael had short unkempt hair, beady eyes, a chiseled face, and prominent cheekbones. He wore a long-sleeved shirt, plain pants, and shoes. 

Plot

Greed Island arc

Sometime after entering the game, Shihael is among a group of new players that are convinced to join Nickes' Alliance, presumably by Contarch. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 14, Chapter 131 Along with the other new recruits, Shihael waits in front of the Shiso Tree for Jispa's arrival. The moment Jispa arrives, he instantly uses an "Accompany" to transport them to Kosofftro, who sits somewhere near the alliance base. As the new recruits are led to the base by Jispa, he divulges to them that their first task is to collect certain spell cards in Masadora for the alliance. At the base, the new recruits are instructed to memorize a list of spell cards within an hour, and Shihael asks a question about the "Fortress" card. 

When the alliance is close to collecting all of the cards in the game, Genthru reveals himself to be the Bomber and claims that he has placed bombs on all of the alliance members. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 15, Chapter 142 After Genthru escapes the alliance base using a "Leave" card, the alliance members begin to panic from the revelation. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 15, Chapter 143 Left with no other options after Jispa's death, the alliance members surrender all of their spell cards to the Bombers, hoping that they will disarm the bombs attached to them. However, in an act of deception, the Bombers simultaneously set off the bombs and kill nearly all of the alliance members. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 15, Chapter 144 

Shihael's name is listed in Gon's binder. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 16, Chapter 158 

Abilities & Powers

Since most of the alliance members joined because they lacked combat abilities, it can be assumed that Shihael also had none. 

Nen

Due to being able to access Greed Island, Shihael was capable of utilizing Nen. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 13, Chapter 121 

Manga Appearances

Trivia

Though Shihael's name is technically given in the manga through Gon's Greed Island binder, the correspondence between the character and his name is only provided in the Hunter's Guide databook. 

Anime and Manga Differences

In the 2011 anime adaptation, Shihael has light colored skin. Hunter × Hunter - Episode 62 (2011)
